caption: The video appears to be a presentation on the topic of "OPS-5G TA2 – Cross-scale 5G Node and Network Security." The presenter, visible in a small inset window, discusses the objective of achieving a usable, scalable "zero-trust" security architecture for devices ranging from IoT sensors to servers. This architecture aims to have minimal impact on size, weight, power (SWaP), and price. The slide illustrates the data paths from User Equipment (UE) to Cloud Service and MEC Service, highlighting the roles of various network components such as the Radio Access Network (RAN), Edge, Core Network (CN), External Network, Internet, and Data Center. The Mobile Edge Cloud (MEC) is emphasized as a key element in this network architecture. Logos of collaborating organizations like Kryptowire, Peraton, and MIT CSAIL are also displayed, indicating their involvement in the project. The presentation is associated with The Linux Foundation, LF Networking, and LF Edge, as indicated by the logos at the bottom.
